entries.definition: To officially end or cancel a law, system, or practice.
entries.example: The government voted to abolish the old tax law.

entries.definition: In or to a foreign country.
entries.example: My friend is going on a summer trip abroad.

entries.definition: Sudden and unexpected, often in an unpleasant way.
entries.example: His abrupt departure left everyone confused.

entries.definition: The state of not being present, or a lack of something.
entries.example: There is an absence of sand in the hourglass.

entries.definition: Not present in a place where someone is expected to be.
entries.example: I was absent from school yesterday because I went on a trip with my father.

entries.definition: Complete or total.
entries.example: I have absolute confidence in your ability to succeed.

entries.definition: To take in and hold a liquid, substance, or information.
entries.example: He used a sponge to absorb the water on the floor.

entries.definition: So entertaining that it holds all your attention.
entries.example: The novel was so absorbing that I read it in one sitting.

entries.definition: Relating to art that expresses ideas or feelings rather than showing realistic images.
entries.example: Abstract art often uses shapes and colours instead of realistic images.

entries.definition: A painting or design that does not represent real objects in a realistic way.
entries.example: The gallery displayed an abstract by Kandinsky near the entrance.

entries.definition: Extremely silly or unreasonable.
entries.example: That group of people making animal noises sounds completely absurd.

entries.definition: Available in very large quantities.
entries.example: Cakes, cookies, and candy were so abundant that the child was very happy.

entries.definition: Existing in large quantities; plentiful.
entries.example: Wildlife is abundant in the national park.

entries.definition: To treat someone or something badly or cruelly on purpose.
entries.example: The mean man abused his dog when it barked too loudly.

entries.definition: Relating to education, especially at colleges and universities.
entries.example: She chose an academic path and went to university.

entries.definition: Good at learning and performing well in school.
entries.example: He was always an academic student who earned top grades.

entries.definition: A teacher or researcher at a college or university.
entries.example: The academic presented her research at an international conference.

entries.definition: Relating to schools and formal education.
entries.example: She was excited to finish high school and enroll as an undergraduate in the fall.

entries.definition: Theoretical rather than practical or relevant to real life.
entries.example: The argument remained academic and had little effect on policy.

entries.definition: A school or institution that offers specialized training or instruction.
entries.example: There are many courses taught at the academy that I go to.

entries.definition: To happen or move at a faster rate, or to make something do so.
entries.example: The driver accelerated to merge onto the motorway.

entries.definition: A way of pronouncing words that shows where someone is from.
entries.example: The new teacher’s accent was clearly a German one.

entries.definition: To agree to take something that is offered.
entries.example: I accepted the girl’s very nice gift.

entries.definition: Agreement that an idea or statement is true, or willingness to receive something offered.
entries.example: The theory gained widespread acceptance among scientists.

entries.definition: The right or ability to enter, reach, or use something.
entries.example: The manager was the only person with access to the password.

entries.definition: An extra item added to something to make it look better or more useful.
entries.example: The store sold colorful accessories like bags, sunglasses, and makeup.

entries.definition: An unexpected event, often causing injury or damage.
entries.example: He damaged the car in an accident.

entries.definition: To have or provide enough space for someone or something.
entries.example: The meeting room can accommodate nine people.

entries.definition: To go somewhere with someone as a companion.
entries.example: My brothers accompanied me to the movie.

entries.definition: To succeed in doing or completing something.
entries.example: He accomplished his goal of running ten miles.

entries.definition: In a way that matches what has been said or done.
entries.example: He feels like he did a good job and his boss should pay him accordingly.

entries.definition: An arrangement with a bank for keeping your money.
entries.example: After I paid for the new car, my bank account was nearly empty.

entries.definition: Required to explain your actions and accept responsibility for them.
entries.example: Political leaders are accountable to the people they represent.

entries.definition: A person whose job is to manage financial records for a business.
entries.example: The accountant helped us keep track of our spending.

entries.definition: The profession or study of managing money and financial records.
entries.example: He works for an accounting firm.

entries.definition: To gather or collect things gradually over time.
entries.example: The mail accumulated in their mailbox while they were on vacation.

entries.definition: Correct and without mistakes.
entries.example: The story in the newspaper wasn’t very accurate.

entries.definition: To say that someone has done something wrong.
entries.example: She accused her brother of breaking her computer.

entries.definition: To get used to something new.
entries.example: I find it difficult to accustom myself to new time zones when I travel around the world.

entries.definition: To feel a continuous dull pain.
entries.example: Mindy’s head ached because she bumped it against the wall.

entries.definition: To succeed in doing something after trying hard.
entries.example: I was happy that I could achieve my goal.

entries.definition: A chemical substance that can burn or dissolve other materials.
entries.example: In chemistry class, we mixed two acids together and watched the reaction.

entries.definition: To accept or admit that something exists or is true.
entries.example: She acknowledged that she had made a mistake.

entries.definition: To thank someone publicly for what they have done or given.
entries.example: The winner acknowledged the support of her family in her speech.

entries.definition: To show that you have noticed someone, for example by greeting them.
entries.example: He acknowledged his colleague with a nod across the room.

entries.definition: To get to know something or someone.
entries.example: Nancy acquainted herself with the new computer.

entries.definition: A person you know slightly but who is not a close friend.
entries.example: She bumped into an old acquaintance at the train station.

entries.definition: To get or obtain something.
entries.example: Tina acquired a strange package yesterday.

entries.definition: Something that someone has gained or bought.
entries.example: Marty was happy with his new acquisition: a very fast bicycle.

entries.definition: A unit of area equal to about 4,047 square metres.
entries.example: They lived on a 150-acre farm.

entries.definition: A performer who does difficult gymnastic or circus tricks.
entries.example: There were acrobats at the circus that did impressive and complicated jumps.

entries.definition: On or to the other side of something.
entries.example: He walked across the board to the other side.

entries.definition: A single thing that someone does.
entries.example: Her kind act helped many people.

entries.definition: Moving around a lot or doing many things.
entries.example: It is important to be active and not sit around all the time.

entries.definition: Real; existing in fact rather than imagined.
entries.example: This is the actual sword that the king owned, not a fake one.

entries.definition: In fact; used to emphasize what is true.
entries.example: My dad looks a little mean, but actually he’s very kind.

entries.definition: Very severe or intense, especially of pain or illness.
entries.example: When she fell out of the tree, the girl felt an acute pain in her arm.

entries.definition: To change your behaviour or ideas to suit a new situation.
entries.example: It took time to adapt to life in a new country.

entries.definition: To change or adjust in order to fit a new situation or environment.
entries.example: When he went to the new town, he had to adapt to all the weather changes.

entries.definition: A person who regularly does or uses something and finds it hard to stop.
entries.example: She was a coffee addict. She had more than three cups each day.

entries.definition: A strong need to keep doing something or using something, especially something harmful.
entries.example: He sought help for his addiction to gambling.

entries.definition: Making someone want to keep doing or using it and unable to stop easily.
entries.example: I think this game is very addictive.

entries.definition: Something extra added to something else.
entries.example: In addition to his job, he also volunteers at the hospital.

entries.definition: The details of where someone lives or works, such as street and town.
entries.example: Please write your address on the form.

entries.definition: Good enough for a particular purpose.
entries.example: This is adequate for my needs.

entries.definition: To act according to a rule, agreement, or belief.
entries.example: If the new employees wish to succeed, they must adhere to the boss’s rules.

entries.definition: Next to or very near something else.
entries.example: My apartment is adjacent to a lovely park and playground.

entries.definition: Located next to or beside something else.
entries.example: Our hotel room was adjacent to the swimming pool.

entries.definition: To be next to or share a border with something else.
entries.example: She can listen to her brother’s conversations because her room adjoins his.

entries.definition: Sharing a border with another room or building.
entries.example: I couldn’t sleep because the people in the adjoining room were loud.

entries.definition: To change something slightly to make it better, more accurate, or more effective.
entries.example: She adjusted the mirror before driving off.

entries.definition: To be responsible for organizing and managing something.
entries.example: The teacher’s assistant will administer the test.

entries.definition: To give someone medicine or medical treatment.
entries.example: The nurse administered the vaccine carefully.

entries.definition: The department or work of managing an organization.
entries.example: She hoped she could be promoted to a job in administration.

entries.definition: Relating to the management of a company or organization.
entries.example: I work as an administrative assistant to the owner of the company.

entries.definition: A person who manages a business or organization.
entries.example: Everyone in the store did whatever the administrator asked them to do.

entries.definition: A high-ranking officer who commands ships in a navy.
entries.example: They won the sea battle because of the admiral’s great leadership.

entries.definition: Permission to enter a place, or the price paid to enter.
entries.example: The admission ticket to the movie was $5.

entries.definition: To allow someone to enter a hospital or other institution for care.
entries.example: Only visitors with a pass were admitted to the ward.

entries.definition: To warn or criticize someone gently for bad behaviour.
entries.example: The teacher admonished Mark because he was chewing gum in class.

entries.definition: A young person between childhood and adulthood.
entries.example: The adolescent was excited about getting a skateboard for his birthday.

entries.definition: To start using an idea, plan, or method.
entries.example: The company adopted a new safety policy.

entries.definition: To legally take another person's child into your family.
entries.example: They decided to adopt a child from overseas.

entries.definition: To love someone or something very much.
entries.example: The children adore their grandmother.

entries.definition: A fully grown person who is no longer a child.
entries.example: My mom and dad are adults.

entries.definition: To move forward.
entries.example: He advanced across the bridge slowly.

entries.definition: Something that helps you do better than others.
entries.example: His height gave him an advantage in basketball.

entries.definition: The arrival or start of something important.

entries.definition: An exciting and unusual experience.

entries.definition: Likely to cause harm or make a situation worse.
entries.example: I worry that the tornado will have an adverse effect on the farm.

entries.definition: To describe or draw public attention to a product or event.
entries.example: The woman started a new company and needed to advertise her products.

entries.definition: An opinion or suggestion about what someone should do.
entries.example: I don’t know how to study for my exams. Can you give me some advice?

entries.definition: To tell someone what you think they should do.
entries.example: My mother often advises people about their money.

entries.definition: Active public support for a cause or idea.
entries.example: Janine’s strong advocacy for the event made people want to attend.

entries.definition: To publicly support a particular cause or policy.

entries.definition: To speak or act in favour of a particular policy or way of doing things.
entries.example: The charity advocates for better mental health services.

entries.definition: Relating to the air or to flying.

entries.definition: Relating to beauty or the appreciation of art and style.
entries.example: The dresses were noteworthy for their aesthetic design.

entries.definition: A formal or special social event.
entries.example: My wife and I attended a formal affair over the weekend.

entries.definition: To produce a change in someone or something.
entries.example: The student’s poor attitude affected the other students in the class.

entries.definition: A feeling of liking or fondness for someone or something.
entries.example: Amanda has a lot of affection for her little sister, Sarah.

entries.definition: To cause someone pain, suffering, or distress.
entries.example: He was afflicted with a rare disease.

entries.definition: Suffering from pain, illness, or distress.

entries.definition: Wealthy; having plenty of money.
entries.example: People in the city are usually more affluent than people in the country.

entries.definition: To have enough money to pay for something.
entries.example: I’ve been saving my money so I can afford to buy a new bike.

entries.definition: A life that some people believe begins when a person dies.
entries.example: I believe that there is an afterlife.

entries.definition: At a later time; after an event.

entries.definition: One more time.
entries.example: Please say that again.

entries.definition: Touching or leaning on something; or opposed to something.
entries.example: They both leaned against the wall.

entries.definition: The number of years someone has lived.
entries.example: She died at the age of 80.

entries.definition: A business or service that acts on behalf of others.
entries.example: I went to a travel agency to help me arrange a flight home.

entries.definition: A plan, goal, or list of things to be done that guides someone's behaviour.
entries.example: The man is pushing his questionable agenda on his colleagues.

entries.definition: A person who works for a company or represents another person.

entries.definition: To make something bad become worse, especially a situation or medical condition.
entries.example: Loud noise can aggravate a headache.

entries.definition: An angry feeling that makes you want to attack or defeat someone.
entries.example: The coach warned the team about unnecessary aggression on the pitch.

entries.definition: Ready or eager to argue or fight; behaving in a forceful way.

entries.definition: To spend a long time worrying and feeling upset about something.

entries.definition: Great physical pain.

entries.definition: A strong unpleasant feeling, especially great worry or sadness.

entries.definition: To have the same opinion or belief as another person.

entries.definition: A formal arrangement or decision about future action.

entries.definition: The work, business, or study of farming.

entries.definition: The growing of plants and raising of animals for food.

entries.definition: In front of or further forward than something else.

entries.definition: To help or support someone.

entries.definition: To cause pain, illness, or trouble to someone.

entries.definition: Sick or in poor health.

entries.definition: A goal that someone wants to achieve.

entries.definition: The invisible gas that surrounds the Earth and that people breathe.

entries.definition: A vehicle that flies in the sky, such as an aeroplane or helicopter.

entries.definition: A company that owns aircraft and carries people or goods by plane.

entries.definition: A place where aeroplanes take off and land.

entries.definition: The passage through which air reaches a person's lungs.

entries.definition: A passage between rows of seats or between shelves.

entries.definition: A warning sound or device that alerts people to danger.

entries.definition: Although; even though.

entries.definition: A type of drink that can make people drunk.

entries.definition: To warn someone about something dangerous or important.

entries.definition: A creature from a different world.

entries.definition: Similar to each other.

entries.definition: Living; not dead.

entries.definition: The whole number or amount of something.

entries.definition: To make something less painful, severe, or serious.

entries.definition: A narrow road or passage between buildings.

entries.definition: To give or set aside something for a particular purpose or person.

entries.definition: To let something happen or give permission for it.

entries.definition: Someone who agrees to help or support another person or group.

entries.definition: Without other people; on your own.

entries.definition: Next to or beside something else.

entries.definition: In a voice that can be heard by others.

entries.definition: A set of letters used for writing a language.

entries.definition: Before now or before a particular time.

entries.definition: In addition; too.

entries.definition: To change something.

entries.definition: At all times; on every occasion.

entries.definition: To surprise someone very much.

entries.definition: Not entirely clear; open to more than one interpretation.

entries.definition: Having a strong desire to be successful, rich, or powerful.

entries.definition: A vehicle that takes sick or injured people to hospital.

entries.definition: To change something to improve it or make it more accurate.

entries.definition: To make changes to a document, law, or agreement, especially to improve it.

entries.definition: In the middle of or surrounded by something.

entries.definition: In the middle of or surrounded by other people or things.

entries.definition: A quantity of something.

entries.definition: More than enough; plentiful.

entries.definition: Enough, and often more than you need.

entries.definition: To make someone laugh or enjoy themselves.

entries.definition: Similar to something else in certain ways.

entries.definition: To examine something in detail in order to understand it.

entries.definition: A relative who lived a long time ago.

entries.definition: A person who presents the news on television or radio.

entries.definition: To present a television or radio programme, especially the news.

entries.definition: Very old; from a long time ago.

entries.definition: Used to connect words, phrases, or clauses.

entries.definition: Again, often in a new or different way.

entries.definition: A strong feeling of being upset or annoyed when something unfair happens.

entries.definition: The direction from which something is viewed; also the space between two lines that meet.

entries.definition: Having a strong feeling of annoyance or hostility.

entries.definition: A living creature that is not a plant.

entries.definition: Having life; living.

entries.definition: The joint that connects the foot to the leg.

entries.definition: A date on which an important past event is remembered each year.

entries.definition: To make something known publicly.

entries.definition: To make someone feel bothered or slightly angry.

entries.definition: Happening once every year.

entries.definition: Calculated or considered over a period of one year.

entries.definition: Happening once every year.

entries.definition: Having an unknown name or identity.

entries.definition: One more of the same kind.

entries.definition: Something said or written in reply to a question.

entries.definition: A small insect that lives in large groups.

entries.definition: A formal or patriotic song, especially one representing a country.
entries.example: Everyone sings the national anthem before the baseball game starts.

entries.definition: The study of people, society, and culture.

entries.definition: A medical drug used to kill bacteria and treat infections.
entries.example: The doctor gave me a shot of an antibiotic when I got the flu.

entries.definition: To expect that something will happen.

entries.definition: A feeling of excitement about something enjoyable that will happen soon.

entries.definition: A substance that stops a poison from causing harm.

entries.definition: Very old and valuable.

entries.definition: An old object, such as furniture or jewellery, that is valuable because of its age and quality.
entries.example: The shop specialises in antiques from the nineteenth century.

entries.definition: A feeling of worry and fear.

entries.definition: Feeling worried or nervous.

entries.definition: Used to refer to one or more things when it does not matter which.
entries.example: Do you have any questions?

entries.definition: Any person at all.

entries.definition: Any thing at all.

entries.definition: Used to change the subject or return to the main point.

entries.definition: In or to any place.

entries.definition: Separated by a distance; not next to each other.

entries.definition: A set of rooms in a building where people live.

entries.definition: A device or set of equipment used for a particular purpose.

entries.definition: Easy to see or understand; obvious.

entries.definition: To be attractive or interesting to someone.

entries.definition: A quality that makes people like or want something.

entries.definition: To seem or give the impression of being something.
